title: austrostipa acrociliata
intro: Austrostipa acrociliata, also known as the Red Hill speargrass, is a species of grass native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective against wounds and infections. The health benefits of Austrostipa acrociliata include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and made into a tea, or used in a topical application to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
